notes: Internal induction — professional floor-care operator and operational readiness in Australian commercial settings. Orderly, onboarding feel, safe and out-of-hours (no children near wet/treated floors). Metric, 240 V/RCD leads, AS/NZS signage. Bright, clean, professional. No on-image text, no real brand names/logos.

images.context: Course hero image for 'Professional Floor Care & Operational Readiness' onboarding
images.prompt: A professional floor-care operator in plain branded workwear, gloves and non-slip footwear operating a walk-behind auto scrubber on gleaming vinyl flooring in a bright, empty Australian commercial corridor after hours. A yellow wet-floor caution sign stands nearby and a cordless power lead is managed tidily. Clean, orderly, aspirational, warm daylight through the windows, controlled work zone. Over-shoulder framing, realistic photography.
images.negativePrompt: no children present, no clutter, no mess, no on-image text, no logos, no brand names, no watermarks, no gore, no distressing/dramatic mould, no courtroom, no identifiable face-forward portraits, no tripping hazards, no imperial units

images.context: Modules on vehicle, machine and equipment readiness and the daily pre-start
images.prompt: The open rear of a fit-out work van with floor-care equipment laid out in orderly readiness: a rotary machine, colour-coded pads, coiled hoses and leads, wet-floor signage, chemical bottles secured upright, PPE and a spill kit, with a gloved hand ticking a clipboard pre-start checklist. Bright daylight, clean and methodical onboarding feel, Australian setting. Mid-distance framing, realistic photography.
images.negativePrompt: no children present, no clutter, no mess, no on-image text, no logos, no brand names, no watermarks, no gore, no distressing/dramatic mould, no courtroom, no identifiable face-forward portraits, no imperial units

images.context: Modules on site setup and hazard control — controlled work zone before the floor is wet
images.prompt: A controlled floor-care work zone being set up in a bright Australian public building foyer: yellow wet-floor A-frame signs and barrier posts cordon off the area, leads and hoses routed clear of the walkway, colour-coded cleaning tools staged neatly, exits kept clear. A gloved operator positions a caution sign. Orderly, safe, professional setup before any water goes down. Mid-distance framing, realistic photography.
